fr.elements: Main Building; Detached Structure; Front Driveway; Landscaped Area; Open Green Space
fr.recommendations: Enhancing the positioning and visibility of the property's single access point could facilitate improved responder access and navigation during an emergency. Addressing the dense vegetation by trimming or removing select areas around the driveway and property perimeter may enhance the visibility and maneuverability for emergency vehicles, allowing for smoother ingress and egress. Additionally, maintaining clear pathways around the main building, detached structure, and landscaped areas could support more efficient movement of personnel and equipment. This strategy may enhance staging operations within the open green space, offering responders better options for setting up operational bases or equipment. Overall, these adjustments could contribute to more effective emergency response activities by ensuring that responders can quickly and safely reach necessary areas on the property.

wildfire.conditions: Scattered Trees; Open Terrain; Defensible Space; Vegetated Perimeter; Limited Side Access; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Scattered Shrubs; Open Fuel Break
wildfire.recommendations: To enhance wildfire resilience and prepare your property for wildfire exposure, consider maintaining the existing open terrain and defensible space by managing scattered trees and shrubs to avoid continuous vegetation clustering. Prioritize vegetation management around the perimeter, especially where vegetation is adjacent to structures, to increase vegetation separation and reduce potential exposure. Focusing on the limited side access, ensure it remains clear and accessible by managing any vegetation that could impede movement or visibility. Maintaining the open fuel break will preserve an essential barrier against fire spread. Continued attention to these areas will support long-term wildfire preparedness by potentially reducing fuel continuity and supporting firefighter access.
